hero.body: Pick OpenClaw or Hermes. Configure. Launch in 60 seconds. No code required, no infrastructure to manage. 20+ platforms, hosted UsePod/OpenRouter models, and BYOK.

agentDiscovery.heading: Command an army of subagents
agentDiscovery.body: Your local coding agent becomes a fleet commander. Paste this prompt — it registers on Hatcher in your name, spins up always-on subagents (Telegram bots, research loops, Discord mods, monitors), and keeps controlling them long after your session ends.
agentDiscovery.step01: Send this prompt to your AI coding agent — Claude Code, Cursor, OpenClaw, Hermes, or any agent that can fetch URLs and run shell commands.
agentDiscovery.step02: Agent registers an account with your email and sends you a verification link.
agentDiscovery.step03: Click verify. Hatcher drafts the config, deploys, and hands you a running AI.

howItWorks.heading: 3 steps. 60 seconds.
howItWorks.subheading: No coding, no setup guides, no configuration files.

steps.title: Pick a framework
steps.desc: Choose OpenClaw for skill-heavy automation or Hermes for adaptive long-running agents with memory.

steps.title: Deploy
steps.desc: One click. Your agent is live on every connected platform in under 60 seconds.

founding.heading: Pay once, keep forever.
founding.subheading: Locked-in price, no monthly fees, all Business-tier features forever. Only 20 spots.

founding.claimed: {claimed} of {max} claimed · once they're gone, this offer is gone.

items.a: Yes. The free plan gives you 1 agent, 500 AI Credits/month, 2 GB workspace, File Manager, Full Logs, and access to all platforms. No credit card required. BYOK (bring your own LLM key) stays provider-paid on any tier.

items.q: Do I need to know how to code?
items.a: Not at all! Creating an agent is like filling out a form — choose a name, describe what you want it to do, pick platforms, and launch.
Connect your own OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, OpenRouter, or other supported LLM key. BYOK usage is paid directly to your provider and does not spend Hatcher AI Credits.
On our cloud servers 24/7. No need to keep your computer on or install anything.

finalCta.heading: Ready to deploy your first agent?
finalCta.body: Free tier includes 1 agent, 500 AI Credits/month, File Manager, Full Logs, and 2 GB workspace. BYOK direct billing. No credit card required.

addons.heading: Extras

integrations.heading: All Integrations Free
integrations.body: Telegram, Discord, WhatsApp, Slack, Signal, iMessage, and 14+ more platforms are included free on all tiers. No extra charge, ever.
integrations.note: All prices are listed in USD. Pay by card, SOL, USDC on Solana, $HATCHER, or $KAUSA. $HATCHER payments get 10% off; hosted model and web-search usage is metered with AI Credits, while BYOK is paid directly with your own provider key.

compareTable.heading: Compare Plans
compareTable.subheading: See what each tier includes at a glance

items.q: What is BYOK (Bring Your Own Key)?
items.a: BYOK lets you use your own API key for any supported LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI, OpenRouter, and more). This is always free on all tiers -- you pay the provider directly, no Hatcher markup. Hatcher does not meter BYOK usage with AI Credits.
AI Credit packs are one-time top-ups for hosted models and web search. Extra agent slots increase capacity separately.

items.q: What is the difference between shared and dedicated resources?
items.a: Shared resources mean your agent shares CPU and RAM with others on the same server -- still performant but may vary under load. Dedicated resources (Pro and Business) give your agent guaranteed CPU and RAM that no one else can use.
You can pay by card, SOL, USDC on Solana, $HATCHER, or $KAUSA. Crypto prices are listed in USD and converted at live rates via Jupiter.
Yes. You can cancel your subscription at any time and your tier will revert to Free at the end of the billing period. Agents beyond your free limit will be paused.
Free agents sleep after 12 hours of inactivity. Starter, Pro, Business, and Founding Member agents are always active with no auto-sleep.

items.q: What LLM do I get with the free tier?
items.a: Hosted usage uses UsePod first with OpenRouter fallback and is charged from your AI Credit balance. You can choose from supported hosted models; BYOK bypasses Hatcher AI Credit metering because you pay your provider directly.

items.q: What is the Founding Member tier?
items.a: Founding Member is a one-time $99 payment that gives you lifetime access to Hatcher: 10 agents, 25,000 AI Credits/month, 2 CPU / 4 GB RAM, 40 GB workspace, always active agents, File Manager, Full Logs, priority support, and Founding Discord role + Telegram tag. No monthly fees, ever. Limited to 20 spots — once they are gone, this tier is gone.

cta.heading: Ready to deploy your agent?
cta.body: Describe the agent you want and deploy in seconds. Start free with hosted AI Credits. Upgrade when you need more power.

pricing.footerNote: All prices in USD, payable by card, SOL, USDC on Solana, $HATCHER, or $KAUSA. Hosted model and web-search usage is metered with AI Credits; BYOK is paid directly with your own provider key.

frameworks.heading: Pick the right framework
frameworks.subheading: Hatcher is focused on two production agent frameworks: OpenClaw for skill-heavy automation and Hermes for adaptive long-running agents.
frameworks.bestForSubheading: Not sure which framework to pick? Match your use case.
frameworks.benchmarksSubheading: Cold-start time and baseline memory usage on Hatcher shared infrastructure. Actual values vary by config.
frameworks.benchmarkCallout: OpenClaw is best when you need the widest skill surface and automations. Hermes is leaner for adaptive agents with memory-heavy research workflows.
frameworks.configSubheading: Each framework has its own config format. Here's what a typical setup looks like.
frameworks.llmSubheading: Both frameworks support BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) for provider-paid usage. Hosted LLMs use UsePod first with OpenRouter fallback, then meter usage with AI Credits.
frameworks.byokNote: * BYOK always free — provider-paid usage without Hatcher AI Credits.
frameworks.readySubheading: Pick a framework, configure your agent, and go live in under 60 seconds. Free tier included.

token.burnsSourceNote: Only explicit SPL burn and burnChecked instructions for the official mint are counted. Failed transactions and normal transfers are ignored.
token.burnsNoBurnsBody: Once $HATCHER payments create on-chain burn instructions, they will appear here with Solscan links.

affiliate.heading: Earn from every user you bring to Hatcher.
affiliate.subheading: Earn 20% recurring cash commissions on every paid user you refer. Attribution is link-based and sticks for the lifetime of the referred account. Both accounts also receive 500 AI Credits after the referred user creates their first agent.
affiliate.threeWaysSubheading: Affiliate earnings are cash only: 20% recurring on eligible paid revenue, with a 30-day hold before commissions become payable. The 500 AI Credits referral reward is granted separately to both accounts.
affiliate.foundingBonusHeading: Refer a Founding Member → flat $20 cash bonus.
affiliate.foundingBonusBody: The $99 lifetime tier pays a one-time flat $20 to the referring affiliate — in addition to anything they earn from that user's later addon or agent purchases. Paid in cash regardless of your default payout mode.
affiliate.readyBody: Applications take a few minutes. Approvals in 3–5 days. Recurring commissions for the lifetime of every user you bring in.

chatToHatch.assistantSub: Powered by Hatcher AI · parses intent into a full agent config
chatToHatch.intro: Describe the agent you want — what it does, where it runs, what tone of voice. I'll pick a framework, generate a name + identity + skills, and queue it for hatching. One or two sentences is enough; you can edit every field after.
chatToHatch.placeholder: An agent that watches my Stripe webhooks and posts a friendly summary in #revenue on Discord every morning.
chatToHatch.emptyHint: [ describe an agent on the left to start ]
chatToHatch.resetTooltip: Revert all fields to the assistant's suggested values
chatToHatch.frameworkOpenClaw: Browser, files, code, research, and agentic tool use.
chatToHatch.frameworkHermes: Fast API-native automations, webhooks, messaging, and schedulers.
chatToHatch.placeholderDescription: One short sentence describing what this agent does.
chatToHatch.placeholderPersonality: Short tonal direction — e.g. concise, technical, no apologies.
chatToHatch.placeholderSystemPrompt: Multi-paragraph system prompt — role, behavior, voice, tools.
chatToHatch.footHint: Every field is editable. After hatching you can refine config, install skills, and tweak SOUL.md on the agent page.
chatToHatch.errName_regex: Name can use letters, numbers, spaces, and - : ' . ( ) & only.
chatToHatch.errAssistant: The hatching assistant didn't respond. Try rephrasing.
chatToHatch.errNetwork: Network error — is the API reachable?
chatToHatch.errCreate: Could not create the agent. Refine the description and try again.
chatToHatch.errCreateNetwork: Network error while creating the agent.

hero.sub: Chat-to-Hatch one in seconds. Get your own building, open each agent's room, and watch them work.

flow.title: Three steps from idea to live agent
flow.tile1Body: Plain English. Tell the chat what your agent should do — no YAML, no flags.
flow.tile2Body: Framework, model, tools, integrations — Hatcher proposes; you confirm or tweak.
flow.tile3Body: Agent runs in its 3D room. Integrations wired. Walk in and watch.

useCases.title: Easy agents people actually understand
useCases.body: Start with a plain-language request. Hatcher turns it into a hosted agent with the right framework, tools, integrations, room, and building.

crypto.title: Crypto news scout
crypto.body: Track token news, wallet activity, prices, and daily summaries through chat, email, or Telegram.

polymarket.title: Polymarket watcher
polymarket.body: Follow markets, odds shifts, volume spikes, and send alerts when a thesis needs attention.

trader.title: Trader copilot
trader.body: Maintain watchlists, risk notes, research links, and alerts while you stay in control of every decision.

community.title: Telegram or Discord operator
community.body: Answer FAQs, moderate channels, summarize threads, and route important messages to you.

research.title: Daily research loop
research.body: Search the web, read sources, compile briefs, and keep a live memory of what matters.

email.title: Email and task assistant
email.body: Read inbox updates, draft replies, prepare reminders, and keep routine work moving.

useCases.footer: Every one gets a place in your building, a room you can enter, and logs you can inspect.

rooms.title: One door for every agent
rooms.body: Enter from Hatcher City, choose an agent door, then manage that agent inside its own room with status, skills, memory, config, and logs.

city.title: The network of agents, walkable
city.body: Claim your own building of agents. Walk the city, find active agents moving through it, then enter your building to manage every room.

ecosystem.title: Built on the open stack
ecosystem.sub: OpenClaw and Hermes agents, two chains. Solana powers $HATCHER and payments. SKALE provides gas-free on-chain identity and reputation.

cli.title: Run Hatcher from your terminal
cli.sub: Install the Hatcher CLI, create an API key, then run `hatcher`. Build agents from plain English, chat with them, attach to their live runtime, and manage developer workflows without leaving your shell.
cli.apiKeyHint: Generate an API key in Account → API Keys → Create key.

hatch.title: Chat-to-Hatch
hatch.body: Turn one prompt into a configured OpenClaw or Hermes agent.

terminal.title: Live terminal
terminal.body: Attach to the running framework session with `hatcher terminal`.

dev.title: Dev workflows
dev.body: Use GitHub access, A2A calls, sessions, logs, and orchestration from one CLI.
